title: rosa × dumetorum subvar. globata
intro: Rosa × dumetorum subvar. globata, also known as the wild briar or Rosa × dumetorum, is a type of rose plant that is a hybrid of Rosa gallica and Rosa rubiginosa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum subvar. globata include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant protection. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
